1	# M55 Charter — CLI-Preflight + Parallel Substrate + Rate-Limit Map + Context Briefs + Verify Gate
2	
3	> Authored: 2026-05-09 15:30 PDT (merged scope) from Desktop conversation alignment + 15:04 in-session definition.
4	> This file is the canonical scope. The unattended supervisor and partition phase consume it.
5	> Archive to `.gsd-t/milestones/m55/charter.md` during complete-milestone.
6	
7	## Context
8	
9	Two pain patterns merged into one milestone:
10	
11	**Pattern A — Silent-skip regressions (from M48/M49/M50/M52 retrofit hooks)**: ad-hoc `Promise.all([Task,Task,...])` patterns scattered across command files bypass `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` so token attribution goes blind on fan-out; "verify" steps report PASS when their precondition checks were never actually run; silent-skip regressions only caught later by retrofit hooks.
12	
13	**Pattern B — Claude rate-limit ceiling at ~3 parallel workers (from Desktop conversation 2026-05-09)**: undocumented ITPM cap, not concurrency cap. Every parallel worker re-loads CLAUDE.md + contracts + domain scope (~60k each) within the same 60s window, tripping the silent cap. Decision: build a CLI-first preflight pattern so most "workers" are deterministic CLIs (zero ITPM) and only the join is an LLM. Stays inside Claude Max — no codex/gemini.
14	
15	Per memory `feedback_claude_max_subscription.md`: local features must use Max subscription, not API key (API key is measurement-only per `feedback_anthropic_key_measurement_only.md`).
16	
17	Per memory `feedback_measure_dont_claim.md`: this milestone has measurable success criteria — not complete until measurement is run AND reported in progress.md + CHANGELOG with numbers.
18	
19	## Goal
20	
21	Lift the practical parallelism ceiling from ~3 LLM workers to ~6–10 mixed workers (1 LLM judge + N CLIs) by replacing deterministic LLM work with deterministic CLI work, AND gate every spawn with deterministic state-precondition checks. Prove via empirical measurement.
22	
23	## Domains (5 file-disjoint — partition will refine task lists)
24	
25	### D1 — State-Precondition Library
26	- `bin/cli-preflight.cjs` — pluggable check registry: `branch-guard`, `ports-free`, `deps-installed`, `contracts-stable`, `manifest-fresh`, `working-tree-state`
27	- Deterministic `{ok, checks[], notes[]}` envelope, schema-versioned, zero deps mirroring `bin/parallelism-report.cjs`
28	- Pure state inspector — no token spend, no LLM calls
29	
30	### D2 — Parallel-CLI Substrate
31	- `bin/parallel-cli.cjs` — N-worker pool runner
32	- Every spawn flows through `captureSpawn` (token-capture invariant)
33	- Log-stream tee to per-worker NDJSON, lifecycle/timeout/fail-fast policy
34	- Engine-only — does NOT touch command files yet
35	- **Concurrency defaults calibrated from D3's empirical map** (D2 depends on D3 output)
36	
37	### D3 — Rate-Limit Probe + Empirical Map (one-shot ~140k token spend, approved)
38	- `bin/gsd-t-ratelimit-probe.cjs` — synthetic-worker harness mirroring real GSD-T spawn shape (Nk context read, Mk output)
39	- Sweep matrix: `parallel_workers ∈ {1,2,3,4,5,6,8} × context_size ∈ {10k, 30k, 60k, 100k}`
40	- Captures per-worker time-to-first-token, 429 count + which worker, total wall-clock, effective ITPM/OTPM achieved
41	- Backoff probe (post-429 recovery window) + steady-state probe (5-min sustained at 3 workers)
42	- Real spawns into throwaway worktree (accuracy matters more than ~140k one-shot token cost)
43	- Output: `.gsd-t/ratelimit-map.json` — account's empirical ceiling. Future spawn decisions consult this.
44	
45	### D4 — Context-Brief Generator (renamed from "preflight" to avoid collision with D1)
46	- `bin/gsd-t-context-brief.cjs` — single entry: `gsd-t brief --kind {execute|verify|qa|red-team|design-verify|scan} --domain X --json`
47	- Produces `.gsd-t/briefs/{spawn-id}.json` (~2k JSON snapshot) replacing the 30–60k context re-read per worker
48	- `.gsd-t/contracts/context-brief-contract.md` v1.0.0 — schema, freshness rules (mtime hash-stamp), fail-open vs fail-closed, allowed CLIs, idempotent-join rule
49	- Workers consume the brief instead of re-reading repo files
50	
51	### D5 — Verify-Gate (two-track) + Wire-In + Doc Ripple
52	- `bin/gsd-t-verify-gate.cjs` — two-track gate:
53	  - **Track 1 (state)**: consumes D1 envelope, hard-fails on any non-`ok` check
54	  - **Track 2 (parallel CLI)**: fans out via D2 substrate — typecheck (tsc), lint (biome/ruff), tests (existing runners), dead-code (knip), secrets (gitleaks), complexity (lizard)
55	  - Returns ≤500-token JSON summary; LLM judges summary, never raw output
56	- Wire-in:
57	  - `gsd-t-execute` Step 1: orchestrator runs D4 context-brief once + D1 state-preflight, workers consume brief instead of re-reading repo
58	  - `gsd-t-verify` Step 2: invoke verify-gate; summary feeds the existing verify report
59	  - Update validation-subagent prompts (`templates/prompts/qa-subagent.md`, `red-team-subagent.md`, `design-verify-subagent.md`) with hard rule: "If you're about to grep/read/run-test, check the brief first."
60	- Doc ripple: `docs/architecture.md` (CLI-Preflight Pattern section), `docs/requirements.md` (REQ-M55-D1..D5-*), `CLAUDE.md` project (mandatory preflight before spawn), `commands/gsd-t-help.md`, `GSD-T-README.md`, `templates/CLAUDE-global.md`
61	- Update Pre-Commit Gate: "brief regenerated if preflight inputs changed"
62	
63	## Domain Dependency Graph
64	
65	```
66	D1 (state checks)        ← independent, can start anytime
67	D3 (ratelimit probe)     ← independent, can start anytime
68	                              ↓ produces ratelimit-map.json
69	D2 (parallel substrate)  ← depends on D3 (calibration)
70	                              ↓ produces parallel-cli.cjs
71	D4 (context briefs)      ← independent of D1/D2/D3 internals; can run parallel with D1 and D3
72	D5 (verify-gate + wire)  ← depends on D1, D2, D4
73	```
74	
75	**Wave plan** (refined at /gsd-t-plan):
76	- Wave 1 parallel: D1 + D3 + D4 (file-disjoint, no internal deps)
77	- Wave 2: D2 (after D3 produces map)
78	- Wave 3: D5 (after D1, D2, D4)
79	- Post-wave: Red Team adversarial QA across all 5 domains
80	- Then test-sync → integrate → verify (dogfoods D5) → measurement → complete-milestone
81	
82	## Falsifiable Success Criteria (8 — measure before tag/publish)
83	
84	Measured against trailing 3 pre-M55 milestones (M52, M54, prior) using `.gsd-t/token-log.md` + `.gsd-t/metrics/`:
85	
86	1. **State-preflight schema published as STABLE contract** — `cli-preflight-contract.md` v1.0.0
87	2. **Substrate proves ≥3× speedup on a real fan-out scenario** via in-tree `bin/m55-substrate-proof.cjs`
88	3. **Verify-gate blocks ≥3 distinct state-preflight failure classes** (wrong-branch, port-conflict, contract-DRAFT) in `e2e/journeys/`-shape tests with `.gsd-t/journey-manifest.json` entries
89	4. **≥40% token reduction per milestone** for execute+verify cycles vs trailing-3 baseline. Numbers in progress.md Decision Log AND CHANGELOG v3.25.10
90	5. **Zero 429 errors at parallelism level D3 declared safe + peak parallelism ≥6 concurrent workers**. Captured in `.gsd-t/metrics/ratelimit-events.jsonl`
91	6. **Verify-gate wall-clock ≤ ½ trailing-3 median**
92	7. **Red Team GRUDGING PASS** — ≥5 broken patches, all caught: preflight-skip-on-error, parallel-substrate-bypasses-capture, verify-gate-falsy-true, branch-guard-typo, contract-staleness-ignored, brief-staleness-ignored
93	8. **Zero regressions on `npm test`** — baseline 2262/2262 unit + 1 documented `event-stream.test.js` env-bleed when run in-session under `gsd-t-milestone`
94	
95	If ANY criterion fails → DO NOT tag, DO NOT publish, recalibrate, document the gap in progress.md, continue iterating. If ALL pass → bump 3.24.10 → 3.25.10 (minor: new feature), tag v3.25.10, npm publish, gsd-t-version-update-all.
96	
97	## Out of Scope
98	
99	- Rewriting all 55 command files to call the substrate directly (separate ratchet milestone, pending M55 ship)
100	- Cross-project preflight/brief propagation via `gsd-t update-all` (follow-up)
101	- Replacing `bin/gsd-t-worker-dispatch.cjs` or `bin/gsd-t-unattended.cjs` with the substrate (those are unattended-mode supervisors, distinct surface)
102	
103	## Standing Directive (survives compaction)
104	
105	Per Desktop alignment 2026-05-09 + reaffirmed 15:30: execute M55 in-session through to COMPLETED. Auto-advance through partition → plan → execute → test-sync → integrate → verify → complete-milestone without stopping. Do not pause for "should I continue?" Only stop for: destructive action, unrecoverable error after 2 fix attempts + debug-loop exit 4, or measurement criteria failure (in which case recalibrate and continue, do not tag).
106	
107	## Resume Hint
108	
109	The unattended supervisor running this charter should treat each domain as a separate spawn (use the supervisor's worker fan-out) and consult this file at every phase boundary to confirm scope hasn't drifted. The 15:04 initial M55 definition is SUPERSEDED by this merged scope.

1	# Domain: m55-d1-state-precondition-library
2	
3	## Responsibility
4	
5	Pluggable state-precondition CLI library — a deterministic, zero-dep, schema-versioned state inspector that emits a `{ok, checks[], notes[]}` envelope. Pure state inspection: no LLM calls, no token spend, no side effects beyond reading filesystem and git state. Mirrors the architectural pattern of `bin/parallelism-report.cjs` (zero deps, deterministic output, JSON envelope).
6	
7	This is the FIRST half of M55's two-track guarantee — every spawn is gated by deterministic state checks before any LLM work begins. The library powers D5's verify-gate Track 1 and D5's `gsd-t-execute` Step 1 wire-in.
8	
9	## Owned Files/Directories
10	
11	- `bin/cli-preflight.cjs` — main library + CLI entry. Exports: `runPreflight({ projectDir, checks?, mode? })` returning `{ ok, schemaVersion, checks: [{ id, ok, severity, msg, details? }], notes: [] }`. CLI form: `node bin/cli-preflight.cjs --project . --json` (or `--text` for human read).
12	- `bin/cli-preflight-checks/` — built-in check registry (one file per check):
13	  - `branch-guard.cjs` — current branch vs CLAUDE.md "Expected branch" guard
14	  - `ports-free.cjs` — required dev ports (configurable list) are unbound
15	  - `deps-installed.cjs` — `node_modules/` exists and `package-lock.json` mtime ≥ `package.json` mtime
16	  - `contracts-stable.cjs` — no contract files in `.gsd-t/contracts/` are flagged DRAFT/PROPOSED when the milestone is past PARTITIONED
17	  - `manifest-fresh.cjs` — `.gsd-t/journey-manifest.json` newer than every file under `e2e/journeys/`
18	  - `working-tree-state.cjs` — git working tree status (clean, allowed-dirty per dirtyTreeWhitelist, unsafe)
19	- `test/m55-d1-cli-preflight.test.js` — unit tests for the library (envelope shape, every check happy/fail path, schema-version stability)
20	- `test/m55-d1-cli-preflight-checks/` — per-check test fixtures (mini repos / git states per check)
21	
22	## NOT Owned (do not modify)
23	
24	- `bin/parallel-cli.cjs` — owned by D2 (only INSPECT for envelope-style reference)
25	- `bin/gsd-t-ratelimit-probe.cjs` — owned by D3
26	- `bin/gsd-t-context-brief.cjs` — owned by D4
27	- `bin/gsd-t-verify-gate.cjs` — owned by D5 (D5 imports D1's `runPreflight`)
28	- Any command file under `commands/` — D5 owns wire-in
29	- `bin/gsd-t.js` — D5 owns the dispatch wire-in (`gsd-t preflight` subcommand)
30	- `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` — read-only reference (preflight is pure, never spawns)
31	- `bin/parallelism-report.cjs` — INSPECT-only reference for envelope idiom
32	
33	## Deliverables
34	
35	- `bin/cli-preflight.cjs` library + 6 built-in checks
36	- `.gsd-t/contracts/cli-preflight-contract.md` v1.0.0 STABLE (envelope schema, check registry shape, severity model, schema-version policy)
37	- ≥1 unit test per check (happy + fail path) plus envelope-shape tests
38	- `gsd-t preflight` subcommand wire-in is **explicitly deferred to D5** to keep D1 file-disjoint
39	
40	## Integration
41	
42	- Track 1 of D5's verify-gate consumes D1's envelope; D1 ships standalone and tested before D5 wires it
43	- D5's `gsd-t-execute` Step 1 wire-in calls `runPreflight()` programmatically

1	# Constraints: m55-d1-state-precondition-library
2	
3	## Must Follow
4	
5	- **Zero runtime deps** — no `require()` of anything outside Node built-ins (`fs`, `path`, `child_process`, `os`). Mirror the discipline of `bin/parallelism-report.cjs`.
6	- **Deterministic output** — same project state in → byte-identical envelope out (sort `checks[]` by `id`, sort `notes[]`).
7	- **Schema-versioned** — envelope's top-level `schemaVersion` field set to `"1.0.0"`. Bump rules documented in `.gsd-t/contracts/cli-preflight-contract.md`.
8	- **Pure** — never spawn child processes that could mutate state. `child_process.execSync` is OK for `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`, `lsof -nP -iTCP:PORT -sTCP:LISTEN`, etc. — read-only commands only.
9	- **Fail-soft per check** — a single check throwing must not crash the library; catch, mark check `ok: false`, set severity, append a `notes[]` entry pointing at the cause.
10	- **Severity model**: `error` (blocks), `warn` (records, does not block), `info` (records, does not block). Top-level `ok` = `false` iff any check is `ok: false` AND `severity === "error"`.
11	- **CLI-as-library-thin-wrapper** — `bin/cli-preflight.cjs` exports the library; the CLI form is a small `if (require.main === module)` block that calls the library and prints JSON or text.
12	- **Per-check files** — one check per file in `bin/cli-preflight-checks/` so adding a check is a single-file change. Registry is a directory scan, not a hand-maintained switch.
13	- **`captureSpawn` exemption** — preflight does not spawn LLMs, so `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` does not apply. Document this explicitly in `cli-preflight-contract.md`.
14	
15	## Must Not
16	
17	- Modify any file outside the Owned scope above
18	- Spawn LLM children (no `claude -p`, no `Task(...)` — preflight is deterministic)
19	- Take a runtime dependency (no `npm install`)
20	- Inline check logic into `bin/cli-preflight.cjs` — every check is a separate file
21	- Reach into `commands/` or `bin/gsd-t.js` (D5's job)
22	- Fail closed when an OPTIONAL check has nothing to inspect (e.g., `manifest-fresh` against a project with no journey manifest → check is `ok: true, severity: info, msg: "no manifest, skipping"`)
23	
24	## Must Read Before Using
25	
26	The execute agent for D1 must read these files BEFORE writing code:
27	
28	- **`bin/parallelism-report.cjs`** — envelope idiom, zero-dep style, JSON-vs-text mode, error-handling discipline. D1's library is structurally a sibling.
29	- **`bin/journey-coverage.cjs`** — pattern for one-shot detector libraries that ship as both lib + CLI; same shape as cli-preflight.
30	- **`scripts/hooks/pre-commit-playwright-gate`** (M50) — example of a state precondition (file mtime comparison) similar to the `manifest-fresh` check.
31	- **`bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs`** — see how `hasUI`/`hasPlaywright` are used as preflight gates today; D1 generalizes that pattern.
32	- **`.gsd-t/contracts/parallelism-report-contract.md`** — sister contract to model `cli-preflight-contract.md` after.
33	- **`CLAUDE.md` (project)** "Expected branch" idiom — how `branch-guard` reads it (markdown grep against the project CLAUDE.md, not the global one).
34	
35	D1 is prohibited from treating any of these as black boxes — read the listed sections before depending on shape.
36	
37	## Dependencies
38	
39	- Depends on: nothing (independent — Wave 1 candidate, runs parallel with D3 and D4)
40	- Depended on by: D5 (Track 1 of verify-gate consumes the envelope; D5's `gsd-t-execute` Step 1 wire-in calls `runPreflight()`)

1	# CLI-Preflight Contract
2	
3	> Status: **PROPOSED** — partition stub. D1 (`m55-d1-state-precondition-library`) promotes to STABLE during execute.
4	> Version: 0.1.0 (stub) → 1.0.0 (STABLE target)
5	> Owner: D1
6	> Consumer: D5 (verify-gate Track 1 + `gsd-t-execute` Step 1 wire-in)
7	
8	## Purpose
9	
10	Pluggable, deterministic, zero-dep state-precondition library. Inspects project state (branch, ports, deps, contracts, manifests, working-tree) and emits a versioned envelope that downstream code judges. Pure inspector — no LLM calls, no token spend.
11	
12	## Envelope (target — D1 finalizes)
13	
14	```json
15	{
16	  "schemaVersion": "1.0.0",
17	  "ok": true,
18	  "checks": [
19	    { "id": "branch-guard",       "ok": true,  "severity": "error", "msg": "...", "details": {} },
20	    { "id": "ports-free",         "ok": true,  "severity": "error", "msg": "..." },
21	    { "id": "deps-installed",     "ok": true,  "severity": "warn",  "msg": "..." },
22	    { "id": "contracts-stable",   "ok": true,  "severity": "warn",  "msg": "..." },
23	    { "id": "manifest-fresh",     "ok": true,  "severity": "info",  "msg": "..." },
24	    { "id": "working-tree-state", "ok": true,  "severity": "warn",  "msg": "..." }
25	  ],
26	  "notes": []
27	}
28	```
29	
30	Top-level `ok` = `false` iff any check is `ok: false` AND `severity === "error"`.
31	
32	## Library API (target)
33	
34	```js
35	const { runPreflight } = require('./bin/cli-preflight.cjs');
36	const result = runPreflight({ projectDir: '.', checks: undefined /* default = all built-ins */, mode: 'json' });
37	if (!result.ok) process.exit(4);
38	```
39	
40	## CLI Form (target)
41	
42	```
43	node bin/cli-preflight.cjs --project . --json
44	node bin/cli-preflight.cjs --project . --text         # human-friendly summary
45	node bin/cli-preflight.cjs --project . --skip ports-free,manifest-fresh
46	```
47	
48	## Built-In Checks (target)
49	
50	| ID | Severity | What it checks |
51	|----|----------|---------------|
52	| `branch-guard` | error | Current branch matches CLAUDE.md "Expected branch" rule |
53	| `ports-free` | error | Required dev ports unbound (configurable list) |
54	| `deps-installed` | warn | `node_modules/` exists; `package-lock.json` mtime ≥ `package.json` mtime |
55	| `contracts-stable` | warn | No `.gsd-t/contracts/*.md` flagged DRAFT/PROPOSED past PARTITIONED milestone state |
56	| `manifest-fresh` | info | `.gsd-t/journey-manifest.json` newer than every file under `e2e/journeys/` |
57	| `working-tree-state` | warn | git working tree clean OR matches `dirtyTreeWhitelist` |
58	
59	## Schema-Version Policy (target)
60	
61	- `schemaVersion` MAJOR bumps for breaking envelope shape changes
62	- MINOR bumps for additive fields
63	- PATCH bumps for documentation/check-text changes only
64	
65	## captureSpawn Exemption
66	
67	Preflight does not spawn LLMs, so `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` does not apply. This contract is the canonical reference for that exemption.
68	
69	## Promotion to STABLE
70	
71	D1 promotes this contract to v1.0.0 STABLE when:
72	- Library + 6 checks shipped
73	- ≥1 unit test per check (happy + fail)
74	- Envelope schema validated by `test/m55-d1-cli-preflight.test.js`
75	- Charter SC1 satisfied

1	# Contract: Parallelism Report
2	
3	**Version**: 1.0.0
4	**Owner**: M44 D9 — parallelism-observability
5	**Status**: Active
6	**Date**: 2026-04-23
7	
8	## Purpose
9	
10	Provide a deterministic, read-only summary of how wide the orchestrator is actually fanning out, drawn from existing persisted state:
11	
12	> "Is the orchestrator actually fanning out across workers, or serializing despite parallelism being available?"
13	
14	> "When this wave finishes, did it hit the parallelism factor D6 estimated?"
15	
16	Pure observability. **Zero added LLM token cost.** The reader derives every metric from files already written by D4/D5/D6 gates, D7 token-log rows, and D8 spawn-plan files.
17	
18	## Public API
19	
20	```js
21	const { computeParallelismMetrics, buildFullReport } = require('./bin/parallelism-report.cjs');
22	
23	const metrics = computeParallelismMetrics({ projectDir: '.', wave: 'wave-3' });
24	// returns the Metrics shape defined below
25	
26	const md = buildFullReport({ projectDir: '.', wave: 'wave-3' });
27	// returns a markdown post-mortem string
28	```
29	
30	Both calls are pure I/O — no LLM, no subprocess, no network.
31	
32	## Metrics shape
33	
34	```json
35	{
36	  "schemaVersion": 1,
37	  "generatedAt": "2026-04-23T22:45:00.000Z",
38	  "wave": "wave-3",
39	  "activeWorkers": 4,
40	  "readyTasks": 6,

# CLI-Preflight Contract

> **Status**: STABLE
> **Version**: 1.0.0
> **Date**: 2026-05-09
> **Owner**: M55 D1 (`m55-d1-state-precondition-library`)
> **Consumer**: M55 D5 (verify-gate Track 1 + `gsd-t-execute` Step 1 wire-in)

## Purpose

Pluggable, deterministic, zero-dep state-precondition library. Inspects project state — branch, ports, deps, contracts, manifests, working-tree — and emits a versioned envelope that downstream code judges. Pure inspector: no LLM calls, no token spend, no side effects beyond reading the filesystem and running a small fixed list of read-only `git` / `lsof` commands. Architecturally a sibling to `bin/parallelism-report.cjs` (zero deps, JSON envelope, schema-versioned, deterministic).

## Public API

```js
const { runPreflight } = require('./bin/cli-preflight.cjs');
const result = runPreflight({
  projectDir: '.',     // required string
  checks: undefined,   // optional Array<id> — default = run all built-ins
  mode: 'json',        // optional 'json' | 'text' — informational; envelope is identical
});
if (!result.ok) process.exit(4);
```

`runPreflight` is synchronous and never throws to the caller. Per-check throws are caught and recorded in the envelope (see § Fail-Soft).

## Envelope (v1.0.0)

```json
{
  "schemaVersion": "1.0.0",
  "ok": true,
  "checks": [
    { "id": "branch-guard",       "ok": true,  "severity": "error", "msg": "...", "details": { } },
    { "id": "contracts-stable",   "ok": true,  "severity": "warn",  "msg": "...", "details": { } },
    { "id": "deps-installed",     "ok": true,  "severity": "warn",  "msg": "..." },
    { "id": "manifest-fresh",     "ok": true,  "severity": "info",  "msg": "..." },
    { "id": "ports-free",         "ok": true,  "severity": "error", "msg": "..." },
    { "id": "working-tree-state", "ok": true,  "severity": "warn",  "msg": "..." }
  ],
  "notes": []
}
```

### Field rules

- `schemaVersion` — string `"MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH"`. v1.0.0 envelope shape locked.
- `ok` — `false` iff at least one check has `ok: false` AND `severity: "error"`. Non-error failures (warn/info) do NOT flip top-level `ok`.
- `checks[]` — sorted ascending by `id` (lexicographic). Stable across runs.
- `checks[].id` — kebab-case, matches the `bin/cli-preflight-checks/<id>.cjs` filename stem.
- `checks[].severity` — `"error" | "warn" | "info"`.
- `checks[].msg` — single-line human summary; required.
- `checks[].details` — optional object with structured payload (e.g., `{expected, actual}` for branch-guard, `{ports: [{port, listener}]}` for ports-free). Omitted when empty.
- `notes[]` — sorted strings. Used for: skipped checks, registry-load failures, per-check throws, missing optional artifacts.

### Severity model

| severity | Effect on top-level `ok` | Use case |
|----------|--------------------------|----------|
| `error`  | Blocks (sets `ok:false`) | Wrong branch, occupied port — never proceed |
| `warn`   | Records, does not block  | Stale lockfile, dirty tree — operator should know |
| `info`   | Records, does not block  | Manifest missing (non-fatal), no expected-branch rule |

## CLI Form

```
node bin/cli-preflight.cjs [--project DIR] [--json | --text] [--skip id1,id2,...]
```

| Flag | Default | Effect |
|------|---------|--------|
| `--project DIR` | `.` | Project root passed to checks |
| `--json` | (default) | Print envelope as `JSON.stringify(envelope, null, 2)` |
| `--text` | off | Print human-readable summary (status icon per check + top-level OK/FAIL) |
| `--skip id,...` | (none) | Skip listed checks; each skip appends a `notes[]` entry `"skipped: <id>"` |

Exit code is `0` when `ok===true` and `4` when `ok===false`. (`4` matches the headless-auto-spawn fail-closed convention used by M50.)

## Built-in Checks

Each check lives in `bin/cli-preflight-checks/<id>.cjs` and exports:

```js
module.exports = {
  id:        'kebab-case-id',           // string, must match filename stem
  severity:  'error' | 'warn' | 'info', // declared severity for this check
  run({ projectDir }) {                  // pure synchronous function
    return { ok: true, msg: '...', details: { /* optional */ } };
  },
};
```

| ID | Severity | Behavior |
|----|----------|----------|
| `branch-guard` | error | Reads project `CLAUDE.md`. Looks for an `Expected branch:` line (anywhere in the file). If found, compares to `git branch --show-current`. `ok:false` if mismatch. If no rule is set, `ok:true` with `msg:"no expected-branch rule set"`. |
| `contracts-stable` | warn | Scans `.gsd-t/contracts/*.md` for `Status: DRAFT` or `Status: PROPOSED`. Reads `.gsd-t/progress.md` to determine whether the project is past PARTITIONED (look for `Status: ACTIVE` or any milestone past PARTITIONED). If past PARTITIONED and any DRAFT/PROPOSED contracts exist, `ok:false`. Otherwise `ok:true`. |
| `deps-installed` | warn | Checks `node_modules/` exists AND `package-lock.json` mtime ≥ `package.json` mtime. `ok:false` if `node_modules/` is missing OR lockfile is older. If neither manifest exists, `ok:true` (non-Node project). |
| `manifest-fresh` | info | Compares `.gsd-t/journey-manifest.json` mtime against every file under `e2e/journeys/`. `ok:false` if manifest is older than any journey file. If either path is missing, `ok:true` with `msg:"no manifest, skipping"` (info-grade noop). |
| `ports-free` | error | Reads `requiredFreePorts: number[]` from `.gsd-t/.unattended/config.json` (default empty). For each port, runs `lsof -nP -iTCP:<port> -sTCP:LISTEN`. `ok:false` if any port has a listener. |
| `working-tree-state` | warn | Runs `git status --porcelain`. Reads `dirtyTreeWhitelist: string[]` from `.gsd-t/.unattended/config.json`. Each dirty path must match the whitelist (simple glob: `**` matches any segments, `*` matches non-slash). `ok:true` iff clean OR all dirty paths whitelisted. |

Adding a new check is a single-file change: drop `bin/cli-preflight-checks/<new-id>.cjs` exporting the shape above; the directory-scan registry will pick it up automatically. No edit to `bin/cli-preflight.cjs` is required.

## Determinism

Same project state in → byte-identical envelope out. Specifically:

- `checks[]` sorted by `id` (lexicographic ascending)
- `notes[]` sorted ascending
- No timestamps in the envelope (timestamps live in caller's logs, not here)
- No PIDs, no random IDs, no environment-dependent strings unless captured under `details`

## Fail-Soft

A single check throwing must not crash the library. The library catches the throw and emits a synthetic check entry:

```json
{ "id": "<id>", "ok": false, "severity": "<declared severity>", "msg": "check threw: <error.message>" }
```

…and appends a corresponding entry to `notes[]`. Top-level `ok` is then computed normally: synthetic failures with `severity:"error"` will set `ok:false`; warn/info synthetic failures will not.

If a check file in the registry is malformed (missing `id`, missing `run`, wrong shape), it is skipped at registry-load time with a `notes[]` entry `"registry: <filename> malformed"`. The library continues with the remaining valid checks.

## Schema-Version Policy

| Bump | Trigger |
|------|---------|
| MAJOR | Breaking envelope shape change (rename a field, change a type, remove a check id from the default registry) |
| MINOR | Additive field on the envelope or on a check entry, new check added to default registry, new severity tier |
| PATCH | Documentation-only changes, check `msg` text changes, internal refactors that preserve byte-identical output |

D1 ships v1.0.0; consumers MAY check `schemaVersion` and refuse to proceed on MAJOR mismatch.

## captureSpawn Exemption

Preflight is pure inspection — it does not spawn LLMs, so the M41 `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` invariant does not apply. The lint at `bin/gsd-t-capture-lint.cjs` does not flag this library. This contract is the canonical reference for that exemption; D5's `gsd-t-verify-gate.cjs` (which DOES spawn an LLM judge) inherits no such exemption.

## Promotion Criteria (satisfied 2026-05-09)

D1 has shipped:

- `bin/cli-preflight.cjs` library + 6 built-in checks under `bin/cli-preflight-checks/`
- ≥1 happy + ≥1 fail unit test per check
- Envelope-shape tests (schemaVersion stability, sort-stability, fail-soft-on-throw)
- CLI integration tests (`--json` parseable, `--text` human-readable, `--skip` honored)

Contract is therefore STABLE v1.0.0. M55 success criterion 1 is satisfied.

## Wire-in (deferred to D5)

The `gsd-t preflight` subcommand wire-in in `bin/gsd-t.js`, plus the `runPreflight()` call inside `bin/gsd-t-verify-gate.cjs` Track 1, are owned by D5 to keep D1 file-disjoint. D5 confirms STABLE flip during its wire-in pass.

git -C /Users/david/projects/GSD-T commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(m55-d1): cli-preflight library + 6 checks + contract STABLE

Pluggable, deterministic, zero-dep state-precondition library mirroring
the bin/parallelism-report.cjs envelope idiom. Pure inspector — no LLM
spawn, no token spend, captureSpawn-exempt per the contract.

- bin/cli-preflight.cjs: runPreflight({projectDir, checks?, mode?}) returns
  {schemaVersion: '1.0.0', ok, checks: [{id, ok, severity, msg, details?}],
  notes: []}. CLI form supports --project, --json, --text, --skip. Exit 0
  on ok / 4 on fail. Directory-scan registry. Sort-stable. Fail-soft on
  per-check throws.
- bin/cli-preflight-checks/: 6 built-in checks
  * branch-guard (error)       — git branch vs CLAUDE.md "Expected branch"
  * ports-free (error)         — lsof against requiredFreePorts
  * deps-installed (warn)      — node_modules + lockfile-mtime freshness
  * contracts-stable (warn)    — DRAFT/PROPOSED contracts past PARTITIONED
  * manifest-fresh (info)      — journey-manifest.json vs e2e/journeys/
  * working-tree-state (warn)  — git status --porcelain vs dirtyTreeWhitelist
- .gsd-t/contracts/cli-preflight-contract.md: PROPOSED v0.1.0 → STABLE
  v1.0.0. Locks envelope shape, severity model, schema-version policy,
  captureSpawn exemption.
- 64 new unit tests (2262 → 2326 total; 3 pre-existing env-bleed failures
  unchanged — zero regressions). >=1 happy + >=1 fail per check; envelope
  shape, sort-stability, fail-soft, CLI integration (--json parseable,
  --text human-readable, --skip honoured, exit-code semantics).

Wire-in deferred to D5 per file-disjointness (D5 confirms STABLE flip
during gsd-t-execute Step 1 and gsd-t-verify Step 2 wire-in).

M55 success criterion 1 satisfied.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

  - `qa.cjs` — pulls qa protocol path (`templates/prompts/qa-subagent.md`), test-runner detection (read `package.json` test script), prior `.gsd-t/qa-issues.md` last 10 entries, contract list. Fail-CLOSED if protocol missing.
  - `red-team.cjs` — pulls protocol path (`templates/prompts/red-team-subagent.md`), recent commits in scope (`git log -10 --oneline`), attack vector seeds (extract from protocol's "broken patches" section if present, else empty array). Fail-CLOSED if protocol missing.
  - `design-verify.cjs` — pulls design contract paths (`.gsd-t/contracts/design*.md` glob), Figma URLs from those (regex extract), screenshot manifest (`.gsd-t/screenshots/manifest.json` if exists). Fail-CLOSED if no design contract present (this kind only fires when there's design work).
  - `scan.cjs` — pulls repo file inventory hash (sha256 of sorted `git ls-files` output), prior scan output mtime (`.gsd-t/scan/output.md` if exists), exclusions (`.gitignore` patterns merged with `.gsd-t/scan/exclusions.txt` if exists). Fail-open default.

  - freshness invalidation (mutate source mtime → brief considered stale on next compare)
  - schema-version stability
  - idempotent-join (run twice with same inputs → byte-identical output, except `generatedAt`)
  - fail-open: missing optional source → `null` field, brief still written
  - fail-closed: missing required source for qa/red-team/design-verify kinds → exit 4 with structured error
  - per-kind happy-path: mini repo fixture (use `os.tmpdir()` scratch dirs) → expected brief shape
  - hard cap enforcement (synthesize an oversize ancillary → expect throw)
  - path-safety rejection (`--domain '../etc/passwd'` → reject)
- T5: Add `.gsd-t/briefs/` line to `.gitignore`. Confirm existing entries are preserved. Also drop a `.gsd-t/briefs/.gitignore` so future builds can rely on the directory existing.
- T6: Run `npm test` and confirm zero regressions. Baseline before D4 (after D1 landed) was 2326 known total / 2323 pass / 3 pre-existing failures. Your work should land at `2326 + N D4` total / `2323 + N D4` pass / 3 pre-existing failures unchanged.
- T7: Single commit `feat(m55-d4): context-brief generator + 6 kinds + contract STABLE`. Pre-Commit Gate: contract STABLE, .gitignore updated, decision-log entry, tests added & passing.

Append exactly one line to `.gsd-t/progress.md` Decision Log section. Use the live system clock (run `node -e "console.log(new Date().toISOString())"` and convert to local PDT for the line) — the date guard hook blocks drift >5 minutes.

```
- 2026-05-09 HH:MM PDT: M55 D4 — `bin/gsd-t-context-brief.cjs` + 6 kinds + `context-brief-contract.md` v1.0.0 STABLE shipped. {N} new tests; suite total {2326+N} / {2323+N} pass / 3 pre-existing failures.
```

- All 6 kind collectors present, each with ≥1 happy + ≥1 fail-or-edge-case test.
- Library passes envelope-shape tests + freshness + idempotent-join + path-safety + hard-cap.
- `node bin/gsd-t-context-brief.cjs --kind execute --domain m55-d1-state-precondition-library --spawn-id smoke-001 --json` succeeds and prints a brief.

- Contract is STABLE v1.0.0.
- `.gitignore` contains `.gsd-t/briefs/`.

content.content:
/Users/david/projects/GSD-T
main

1	# M55 Charter — CLI-Preflight + Parallel Substrate + Rate-Limit Map + Context Briefs + Verify Gate
2	
3	> Authored: 2026-05-09 15:30 PDT (merged scope) from Desktop conversation alignment + 15:04 in-session definition.
4	> This file is the canonical scope. The unattended supervisor and partition phase consume it.
5	> Archive to `.gsd-t/milestones/m55/charter.md` during complete-milestone.
6	
7	## Context
8	
9	Two pain patterns merged into one milestone:
10	
11	**Pattern A — Silent-skip regressions (from M48/M49/M50/M52 retrofit hooks)**: ad-hoc `Promise.all([Task,Task,...])` patterns scattered across command files bypass `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` so token attribution goes blind on fan-out; "verify" steps report PASS when their precondition checks were never actually run; silent-skip regressions only caught later by retrofit hooks.
12	
13	**Pattern B — Claude rate-limit ceiling at ~3 parallel workers (from Desktop conversation 2026-05-09)**: undocumented ITPM cap, not concurrency cap. Every parallel worker re-loads CLAUDE.md + contracts + domain scope (~60k each) within the same 60s window, tripping the silent cap. Decision: build a CLI-first preflight pattern so most "workers" are deterministic CLIs (zero ITPM) and only the join is an LLM. Stays inside Claude Max — no codex/gemini.
14	
15	Per memory `feedback_claude_max_subscription.md`: local features must use Max subscription, not API key (API key is measurement-only per `feedback_anthropic_key_measurement_only.md`).
16	
17	Per memory `feedback_measure_dont_claim.md`: this milestone has measurable success criteria — not complete until measurement is run AND reported in progress.md + CHANGELOG with numbers.
18	
19	## Goal
20	
21	Lift the practical parallelism ceiling from ~3 LLM workers to ~6–10 mixed workers (1 LLM judge + N CLIs) by replacing deterministic LLM work with deterministic CLI work, AND gate every spawn with deterministic state-precondition checks. Prove via empirical measurement.
22	
23	## Domains (5 file-disjoint — partition will refine task lists)
24	
25	### D1 — State-Precondition Library
26	- `bin/cli-preflight.cjs` — pluggable check registry: `branch-guard`, `ports-free`, `deps-installed`, `contracts-stable`, `manifest-fresh`, `working-tree-state`
27	- Deterministic `{ok, checks[], notes[]}` envelope, schema-versioned, zero deps mirroring `bin/parallelism-report.cjs`
28	- Pure state inspector — no token spend, no LLM calls
29	
30	### D2 — Parallel-CLI Substrate
31	- `bin/parallel-cli.cjs` — N-worker pool runner
32	- Every spawn flows through `captureSpawn` (token-capture invariant)
33	- Log-stream tee to per-worker NDJSON, lifecycle/timeout/fail-fast policy
34	- Engine-only — does NOT touch command files yet
35	- **Concurrency defaults calibrated from D3's empirical map** (D2 depends on D3 output)
36	
37	### D3 — Rate-Limit Probe + Empirical Map (one-shot ~140k token spend, approved)
38	- `bin/gsd-t-ratelimit-probe.cjs` — synthetic-worker harness mirroring real GSD-T spawn shape (Nk context read, Mk output)
39	- Sweep matrix: `parallel_workers ∈ {1,2,3,4,5,6,8} × context_size ∈ {10k, 30k, 60k, 100k}`
40	- Captures per-worker time-to-first-token, 429 count + which worker, total wall-clock, effective ITPM/OTPM achieved
41	- Backoff probe (post-429 recovery window) + steady-state probe (5-min sustained at 3 workers)
42	- Real spawns into throwaway worktree (accuracy matters more than ~140k one-shot token cost)
43	- Output: `.gsd-t/ratelimit-map.json` — account's empirical ceiling. Future spawn decisions consult this.
44	
45	### D4 — Context-Brief Generator (renamed from "preflight" to avoid collision with D1)

60	- Doc ripple: `docs/architecture.md` (CLI-Preflight Pattern section), `docs/requirements.md` (REQ-M55-D1..D5-*), `CLAUDE.md` project (mandatory preflight before spawn), `commands/gsd-t-help.md`, `GSD-T-README.md`, `templates/CLAUDE-global.md`

1	# Domain: m55-d4-context-brief-generator
2	
3	## Responsibility
4	
5	Replace the 30–60k context re-read every parallel worker performs (CLAUDE.md + contracts + domain scope + relevant code) with a single ~2k JSON snapshot generated once per spawn. The brief becomes the worker's first-and-only source of truth — workers grep the brief instead of re-walking the repo. This is the dominant ITPM-relief lever in the M55 charter (Pattern B from charter Context).
6	
7	## Owned Files/Directories
8	
9	- `bin/gsd-t-context-brief.cjs` — main library + CLI entry. Public CLI: `gsd-t brief --kind {execute|verify|qa|red-team|design-verify|scan} --domain X --json` (or write-to-file mode `--out .gsd-t/briefs/{spawnId}.json`). Library exports: `generateBrief({projectDir, kind, domain, spawnId})` returning the full JSON snapshot.
10	- `bin/gsd-t-context-brief-kinds/` — one file per `kind`, each exporting `{ name, collect(ctx) }` where `collect` returns the kind-specific subset of fields. Mirrors D1's per-check directory pattern.
11	  - `execute.cjs` — domain scope, constraints, contracts referenced, files-owned list, branch state, expected outputs
12	  - `verify.cjs` — verify-gate plan, prior verify results, current contract DRAFT/STABLE state, success criteria
13	  - `qa.cjs` — qa protocol path, test runner detection, prior qa-issues entries, contract list
14	  - `red-team.cjs` — red-team protocol path, recent commits in scope, attack vector seeds
15	  - `design-verify.cjs` — design contract path(s), Figma URL(s) if present, screenshot manifest
16	  - `scan.cjs` — repo file inventory hash, prior scan output mtime, exclusions
17	- `.gsd-t/briefs/` — output directory, one JSON file per spawn (`{spawn-id}.json`). Created on first write; `.gitignore` -ed.
18	- `.gsd-t/contracts/context-brief-contract.md` v1.0.0 STABLE — schema, freshness rules (mtime hash-stamp invalidates brief if any source file's mtime > brief mtime), fail-open vs fail-closed (default fail-open: stale brief warns, doesn't block), idempotent-join rule (re-generating with the same inputs yields byte-identical output).
19	- `test/m55-d4-context-brief.test.js` — unit tests (kind dispatch, freshness invalidation, schema-version, idempotent-join, fail-open-on-missing-source).
20	- `test/m55-d4-context-brief-kinds/` — per-kind test fixtures (mini repo states for each kind's `collect` path).
21	
22	## NOT Owned (do not modify)
23	
24	- `bin/cli-preflight.cjs` — D1
25	- `bin/parallel-cli.cjs` — D2
26	- `bin/gsd-t-ratelimit-probe.cjs` — D3
27	- `bin/gsd-t-verify-gate.cjs` — D5 (D5 imports D4's `generateBrief`)

30	- `templates/prompts/design-verify-subagent.md` — D5 owns

34	
35	## Deliverables
36	
37	- `bin/gsd-t-context-brief.cjs` library + 6 per-kind collectors
38	- `.gsd-t/contracts/context-brief-contract.md` v1.0.0 STABLE
39	- `.gsd-t/briefs/` directory created + added to `.gitignore`

42	
43	## Brief Schema (Charter Sketch)
44	
45	```json
46	{
47	  "schemaVersion": "1.0.0",
48	  "generatedAt": "ISO8601",
49	  "spawnId": "string",
50	  "kind": "execute|verify|qa|red-team|design-verify|scan",
51	  "domain": "string|null",
52	  "sourceMtimes": { "<path>": "ISO8601" },
53	  "branch": "string",
54	  "contracts": [{ "path": "string", "status": "STABLE|DRAFT|PROPOSED" }],
55	  "scope": { "owned": ["..."], "notOwned": ["..."], "deliverables": ["..."] },
56	  "constraints": ["..."],
57	  "ancillary": { "<kind-specific>": "..." }
58	}
59	```
60	
61	Target size: ≤2k JSON per brief. If a kind needs more, the kind file is responsible for hashing/summarizing — never inlining raw 30k contracts.
62	
63	## Integration
64	
65	- D5's `gsd-t-execute` Step 1 wire-in calls `generateBrief()` once per spawn batch and writes to `.gsd-t/briefs/{spawnId}.json`. Workers receive the brief path as part of their prompt scaffold.
66	- D5 also adds the "if you're about to grep/read/run-test, check the brief first" hard-rule line to qa/red-team/design-verify subagent protocols.
67	- The brief is **not** the verify-gate input — the verify-gate uses D1's preflight envelope (state) + D2's parallel CLI summaries (CLI runs). D4's brief is for LLM context relief.
68	
69	## Sequencing
70	
71	- Wave 1 — independent of D1, D2, D3 internals. Can start immediately, parallel with D1 and D3.
72	- Outputs the brief library + contract; D5 (Wave 3) wires it into commands and subagent protocols.
73

1	# Constraints: m55-d4-context-brief-generator
2	
3	## Must Follow
4	
5	- **Zero runtime deps** — only Node built-ins (`fs`, `path`, `child_process`, `crypto`). Mirror D1 / `parallelism-report.cjs` discipline.
6	- **Pure** — brief generation reads filesystem and `git` state; never spawns LLMs, never mutates anything except `.gsd-t/briefs/{spawnId}.json`.
7	- **Deterministic / idempotent-join** — same `(projectDir, kind, domain, spawnId, sourceMtimes)` → byte-identical brief. Sort all arrays. Stable JSON keys.
8	- **Schema-versioned** — `schemaVersion: "1.0.0"` at the top level. Contract documents bump rules.
9	- **Per-kind file** — adding a new kind is a single-file change in `bin/gsd-t-context-brief-kinds/`. Registry is a directory scan, not a switch.
10	- **Size budget** — each brief targets ≤2k JSON bytes. Kind collectors that exceed must summarize (e.g., contract file → `{ path, status, sha256-prefix }`, NOT `{ path, fullText }`).
11	- **Freshness via mtime hash-stamp** — every source file the brief references is recorded with its mtime; verify-time check fails-open (warn) by default, fail-closed if `--strict` flag passed.
12	- **Fail-open default** — a missing optional source file (e.g., no Figma URL for non-design kinds) yields a brief field of `null`, never a crash. Required-source absence (e.g., domain dir doesn't exist) yields a structured error object the caller can decide to fail-closed on.
13	- **`captureSpawn` exemption** — brief generation does not spawn LLMs, so `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` does not apply. Document this explicitly in `context-brief-contract.md`.
14	- **`.gsd-t/briefs/` is gitignored** — briefs are per-spawn ephemera, not committed artifacts.
15	
16	## Must Not
17	
18	- Modify any file outside the Owned scope above
19	- Spawn LLM children (`claude -p`, `Task(...)`)
20	- Take a runtime dependency
21	- Inline kind logic into `bin/gsd-t-context-brief.cjs` — every kind is a separate file
22	- Reach into `commands/` or `bin/gsd-t.js` (D5's job)
23	- Inline raw contract / CLAUDE.md text into the brief — summarize and hash-stamp
24	- Crash on missing optional inputs — fail-open + structured `null` field
25	
26	## Must Read Before Using
27	
28	The execute agent for D4 must read these files BEFORE writing code:
29	
30	- **`bin/journey-coverage.cjs`** — pattern for one-shot detector libraries that ship as both lib + CLI; D4's library is structurally a sibling.
31	- **`bin/parallelism-report.cjs`** — envelope idiom + zero-dep style.
32	- **`.gsd-t/contracts/parallelism-report-contract.md`** — sister contract to model `context-brief-contract.md` after.
33	- **`bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs`** — see how the spawn scaffold currently injects context; D4's brief replaces a chunk of that.
34	- **The 6 subagent protocols** in `templates/prompts/` — `qa-subagent.md`, `red-team-subagent.md`, `design-verify-subagent.md` — D4 reads them to author each `kind`'s collector accurately. D4 does NOT modify these (D5 does).
35	- **Existing domain scope/constraints/tasks files** — D4 reads the structure once to confirm the `execute` kind collector reproduces the right slice.
36	
37	D4 is prohibited from treating any of these as black boxes — read the listed sections before depending on shape.
38	
39	## Dependencies
40	
41	- Depends on: nothing internal — independent (Wave 1 candidate, runs parallel with D1 and D3)
42	- Depended on by: D5 (wires the brief into command Step 1 + subagent protocols)
43

1	# Tasks: m55-d4-context-brief-generator
2	
3	**Domain wave**: Wave 1 (parallel with D1 + D3)
4	**Depends on**: nothing internal — independent
5	**Depended on by**: D5 (wires brief into commands + subagent protocols)
6	
7	## T1 — Author `context-brief-contract.md` v1.0.0 STABLE
8	
9	**Output**: `.gsd-t/contracts/context-brief-contract.md`
10	**Acceptance**: schema, freshness rules (mtime hash-stamp invalidation), fail-open default vs `--strict` fail-closed, idempotent-join rule (byte-identical output for same inputs), kind registry shape, ≤2k size budget per brief, allowed CLI summarization patterns, captureSpawn-exemption note.
11	
12	## T2 — Implement `bin/gsd-t-context-brief.cjs` library + thin CLI
13	
14	**Output**: `bin/gsd-t-context-brief.cjs`
15	**Acceptance**: zero deps; exports `generateBrief({projectDir, kind, domain, spawnId})`; CLI form `gsd-t brief --kind X --domain Y --json` (stdout) or `--out path` (file); registry is directory-scan of `bin/gsd-t-context-brief-kinds/*.cjs`; deterministic-output (sorted arrays, stable keys); fail-open on missing optional source (null field), structured error on missing required source.
16	
17	## T3 — Implement 6 kind collectors under `bin/gsd-t-context-brief-kinds/`
18	
19	**Output**: 6 files: `execute.cjs`, `verify.cjs`, `qa.cjs`, `red-team.cjs`, `design-verify.cjs`, `scan.cjs`
20	**Acceptance**: each exports `{ name, collect(ctx) }` returning a kind-specific object that fits inside the ≤2k brief budget. execute pulls domain scope/constraints/contracts/files-owned/branch/expected-outputs. verify pulls verify-gate plan + prior results + contract DRAFT/STABLE state + success criteria. qa pulls qa protocol path + test runner detection + qa-issues. red-team pulls protocol + recent commits + attack vector seeds. design-verify pulls design contract path(s) + Figma URL(s) + screenshot manifest. scan pulls repo file inventory hash + prior scan output mtime + exclusions.
21	
22	## T4 — Author `test/m55-d4-context-brief.test.js` + per-kind fixture tests
23	
24	**Output**: `test/m55-d4-context-brief.test.js` + `test/m55-d4-context-brief-kinds/*.test.js` (one per kind)
25	**Acceptance**: kind-dispatch test, freshness-invalidation test (mutate source mtime → brief stale), schema-version test, idempotent-join test (run twice → byte-identical), fail-open test (missing optional source → null field), per-kind happy-path test (mini repo fixture → expected brief shape).
26	
27	## T5 — Add `.gsd-t/briefs/` to `.gitignore`
28	
29	**Output**: `.gitignore` additive line: `.gsd-t/briefs/`
30	**Acceptance**: line present; existing `.gitignore` entries preserved.
31	
32	## T6 — Run full test suite; confirm zero regressions
33	
34	**Output**: green `npm test`
35	**Acceptance**: baseline 2262/2262 preserved + D4 tests added.
36	
37	## T7 — Commit D4
38	
39	**Output**: single commit `feat(m55-d4): context-brief generator + 6 kinds + contract STABLE`
40	**Acceptance**: Pre-Commit Gate passes; `.gitignore` updated; brief library + 6 kinds + contract committed.
41	
42	## D4 Checkpoint
43	
44	After T1–T7: D4 ready for D5 to wire. STABLE contract published. Wave 1 contribution complete.
45

1	# Context-Brief Contract
2	
3	> Status: **PROPOSED** — partition stub. D4 (`m55-d4-context-brief-generator`) promotes to STABLE during execute.
4	> Version: 0.1.0 (stub) → 1.0.0 (STABLE target)
5	> Owner: D4
6	> Consumer: D5 (`gsd-t-execute` Step 1 wire-in; subagent prompts read brief path from env)
7	
8	## Purpose
9	
10	Replace the 30–60k context re-read every parallel worker performs (CLAUDE.md + contracts + scope + relevant code) with a ~2k JSON snapshot generated once per spawn. Workers grep the brief instead of re-walking the repo. The dominant ITPM-relief lever in M55.
11	
12	## Library API (target)
13	
14	```js
15	const { generateBrief } = require('./bin/gsd-t-context-brief.cjs');
16	const brief = generateBrief({ projectDir: '.', kind: 'execute', domain: 'm55-d1-state-precondition-library', spawnId: 'execute-D1-T3-...Z' });
17	fs.writeFileSync(`.gsd-t/briefs/${brief.spawnId}.json`, JSON.stringify(brief, null, 2));
18	```
19	
20	## CLI Form (target)
21	
22	```
23	gsd-t brief --kind execute --domain m55-d1-state-precondition-library --json
24	gsd-t brief --kind qa --domain m55-d3-ratelimit-probe-map --out .gsd-t/briefs/qa-...json
25	```
26	
27	## Schema (target — D4 finalizes)
28	
29	```json
30	{
31	  "schemaVersion": "1.0.0",
32	  "generatedAt": "2026-05-NNTNN:NN:NNZ",
33	  "spawnId": "string",
34	  "kind": "execute|verify|qa|red-team|design-verify|scan",
35	  "domain": "string|null",
36	  "sourceMtimes": { "<path>": "ISO8601" },
37	  "branch": "string",
38	  "contracts": [{ "path": "string", "status": "STABLE|DRAFT|PROPOSED" }],
39	  "scope": { "owned": ["..."], "notOwned": ["..."], "deliverables": ["..."] },
40	  "constraints": ["..."],
41	  "ancillary": { "<kind-specific>": "..." }
42	}
43	```
44	
45	## Hard Token Cap
46	
47	Every brief is ≤2,500 tokens (≈10 KB JSON). Validation at write-time. Over-cap is a runtime error, not silent truncation.
48	
49	## Freshness via mtime hash-stamp
50	
51	`sourceMtimes` records every file the brief depends on. A consumer regenerates if any `sourceMtimes[path]` has changed (mtime newer than recorded value). Default behavior is fail-open (warn) when source is stale; `--strict` flag fails closed.
52	
53	## Idempotent-Join
54	
55	Same `(projectDir, kind, domain, spawnId, sourceMtimes)` → byte-identical brief output. Sort all arrays. Stable JSON keys. `generatedAt` is the only varying field on re-runs.
56	
57	## Fail-Open vs Fail-Closed
58	
59	- `--kind execute|verify|scan` → **fail-open** by default (missing optional source → field set to `null`, brief still written)
60	- `--kind qa|red-team|design-verify` → **fail-closed** (missing required source → exit 4 with structured `notes[]`)
61	
62	## captureSpawn Exemption
63	
64	Brief generation is pure (filesystem + git read; no LLM spawn), so `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` does not apply.
65	
66	## Path Safety
67	
68	`--domain` and `--spawnId` accept only `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+`. Any `..` or `/` rejects with non-zero exit.
69	
70	## `.gsd-t/briefs/` is gitignored
71	
72	Briefs are per-spawn ephemera. D4 ships `.gsd-t/briefs/.gitignore` containing `*` (or equivalent global rule).
73	
74	## Promotion to STABLE
75	
76	D4 promotes to v1.0.0 STABLE when:
77	- Library + 6 kind collectors shipped
78	- ≥1 unit test per kind plus envelope/freshness/idempotent-join
79	- Charter-aligned schema validated by `test/m55-d4-context-brief.test.js`
80

1	# QA Subagent Prompt — Per-Task Validation
2	
3	You are the QA agent. Your sole job is test generation, execution, and gap reporting. You write ZERO feature code. You never modify implementation files — only test files and reports.
4	
5	## What to Do
6	
7	1. **Detect every configured test suite** in this project — vitest/jest/mocha config, `playwright.config.*`, `cypress.config.*`. Run EVERY suite that exists.
8	2. **Run the full unit suite.** Report exact pass/fail counts.
9	3. **Run the full E2E suite if any E2E config exists.** Skipping E2E because "the task didn't touch the UI" is a QA FAILURE — every task runs the full suite.
10	4. **Read `.gsd-t/contracts/`** for contract definitions. For every contract referenced by the task, verify the implementation matches the contract shape exactly (API response shape, schema, component props, error format).
11	5. **Audit E2E test quality.** Walk every Playwright spec. If any spec only checks element existence (`isVisible`, `toBeAttached`, `toBeEnabled`, `toHaveCount`) without verifying functional behavior (state changes, data loaded, content updated after user actions, navigation reaches new content), flag it as `SHALLOW TEST — needs functional assertions`. A passing test suite that doesn't catch broken features is a QA FAILURE.
12	6. **Validate Stack Rules compliance** if Stack Rules were injected for the work subagent. Stack rule violations have the same severity as contract violations.
13	
14	## Exploratory Testing (only if Playwright MCP is available)
15	
16	After all scripted tests pass:
17	1. Check whether Playwright MCP is registered in Claude Code settings (look for "playwright" in mcpServers).
18	2. If available: spend 3 minutes on interactive exploration via Playwright MCP — try variations of happy paths with unexpected inputs, probe for race conditions, double-submits, and empty states; test keyboard navigation.
19	3. Tag findings `[EXPLORATORY]` in your report and append them to `.gsd-t/qa-issues.md` with the same prefix.
20	4. If Playwright MCP is not available, skip this section silently. Exploratory findings do NOT count against scripted pass/fail counts.
21	
22	## Report Format (exact)
23	
24	`Unit: X/Y pass | E2E: X/Y pass (or N/A if no config) | Contract: compliant/N violations | Shallow tests: N (list) | Stack rules: compliant/N violations`
25	
26	Append every issue found to `.gsd-t/qa-issues.md` using the existing column schema. If QA fails OR shallow tests are present, do NOT mark the task complete — return a FAIL verdict so the orchestrator can spawn a fix cycle.
27

1	# Red Team Subagent Prompt — Adversarial QA (per-domain)
2	
3	You are a Red Team QA adversary. Your job is to BREAK the code that was just written for this domain. You operate with inverted incentives — your value is measured by REAL bugs found, not tests passed.
4	
5	## Hard Rules
6	
7	- **Bugs found = value.** A short attack list is failure.
8	- **False positives DESTROY your credibility.** Never report something you have not reproduced. A bug is `I did X, expected Y, got Z` with proof.
9	- Style opinions are NOT bugs. Theoretical concerns are NOT bugs.
10	- You are done ONLY when you have exhausted every category below — either find a real bug or document exactly what you tried and why it didn't break.
11	
12	## Attack Categories (exhaust ALL)
13	
14	1. **Contract Violations** — Read `.gsd-t/contracts/`. Does the code match every contract exactly? Test each endpoint/interface/schema shape.
15	2. **Boundary Inputs** — empty strings, null, undefined, huge payloads, special characters, SQL injection, XSS, path traversal.
16	3. **State Transitions** — actions out of order, double-submit, concurrent access, refresh mid-flow.
17	4. **Error Paths** — remove env vars, kill the database, send malformed requests. Does the code degrade gracefully or crash?
18	5. **Missing Flows** — Read `docs/requirements.md`. Are there user flows that exist in requirements but have no test coverage?
19	6. **Regression** — Run the FULL test suite. Did any existing test break?
20	7. **E2E Functional Gaps** — Review every Playwright spec. Are they testing real behavior or just checking element existence? Flag and rewrite shallow specs.
21	8. **Design Fidelity** (only if `.gsd-t/contracts/design-contract.md` exists) — see `design-verify-subagent.md`. The design verification agent runs this attack category as a separate dedicated agent; do not duplicate its work, but flag any design-related bug you incidentally find.
22	
23	## Exploratory Testing (only if Playwright MCP is available)
24	
25	Spend 5 minutes on adversarial interactive exploration via Playwright MCP — race conditions, double-submits, concurrent access, rapid state transitions, error recovery. Tag findings `[EXPLORATORY]`. Skip silently if MCP is not available.
26	
27	## Report Format
28	
29	For each bug:
30	- **BUG-{N}**: severity CRITICAL | HIGH | MEDIUM | LOW
31	  - **Reproduction**: exact steps
32	  - **Expected**: what should happen
33	  - **Actual**: what does happen
34	  - **Proof**: test file or command that demonstrates the bug
35	
36	Summary:
37	- BUGS FOUND: {count} with severity breakdown
38	- COVERAGE GAPS: {untested flows from requirements}
39	- SHALLOW TESTS REWRITTEN: {count}
40	- CONTRACTS VERIFIED: {N}/{total}
41	- ATTACK VECTORS TRIED: every category attempted, each with one-line result
42	- VERDICT: `FAIL` ({N} bugs found) | `GRUDGING PASS` (exhaustive search, nothing found)
43	
44	Write findings to `.gsd-t/red-team-report.md`. If bugs found, also append to `.gsd-t/qa-issues.md`.
45	
46	## Test Pass-Through — Journey Edition (M52)
47	
48	**Activates when**: `.gsd-t/journey-manifest.json` exists AND `e2e/journeys/` is non-empty (M52 D2 has landed).
49	
50	**Goal**: Prove the journey specs catch real regressions in the journeys they
51	claim to cover. A journey spec that only checks "the button exists and is
52	clickable" passes through any breakage to the user — that is what this
53	category attacks.
54	
55	**Protocol**:
56	
57	1. For each spec in `.gsd-t/journey-manifest.json`, identify the listener(s) it covers.
58	2. Write a deliberately-broken patch to `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html` that
59	   targets that listener — examples:
60	   - Remove the listener entirely (`addEventListener` line stripped).
61	   - Comment out the side-effect inside the handler (e.g. `_ssSet` call).
62	   - Swap a sessionStorage key name (e.g. splitterPct key → `'XXX'`).
63	   - Stub the handler to early-return (`if (true) return;` at top).
64	   - Reverse a state mutation (`next ? 'true' : 'false'` → `next ? 'false' : 'true'`).
65	3. Run the journey spec against the broken viewer.
66	4. **PASS**: spec FAILS (red) → revert patch → spec PASSES (green). Record `caught`.
67	5. **FAIL**: spec PASSES with broken viewer → SHALLOW SPEC, must be tightened.
68	   Record `pass-through` and rewrite the assertion to verify state change /
69	   data flow / content load.
70	6. Write at least 5 broken patches across different specs. Each pass-through
71	   is a verdict-level FAIL until rewritten.
72	
73	**Hook end-to-end exercise** (also part of this category):
74	- Stage a viewer-source diff that adds a NEW listener with no manifest entry.
75	- Confirm `pre-commit-journey-coverage` blocks the commit (exit 1).
76	- Update `.gsd-t/journey-manifest.json` with a covering entry.
77	- Confirm the hook now allows the commit (exit 0).
78	- Both transitions logged in `.gsd-t/red-team-report.md` § "M52 JOURNEY-EDITION RED TEAM".
79	
80	**Findings format** in `.gsd-t/red-team-report.md` (append-only section):

1	# Design Verification Subagent Prompt — Per-Domain Visual Audit
2	
3	You are the Design Verification Agent. Your ONLY job is to visually compare the built frontend against the original design and produce a structured comparison table. You write ZERO feature code. Your sole deliverable is the comparison table and verification results.
4	
5	**FAIL-BY-DEFAULT.** Every visual element starts UNVERIFIED. You must prove each one matches — never assume. "Looks close" is not a verdict. "Appears to match" is not a verdict. The only valid verdicts are `✅ MATCH` (with proof) or `❌ DEVIATION` (with specific values).
6	
7	## Step 0: Element Count Reconciliation (run BEFORE anything else)
8	
9	A missing widget is the easiest deviation to miss in a 30-row comparison table — and the most catastrophic.
10	
11	1. Read `INDEX.md` (hierarchical) or `design-contract.md` (flat) to get Figma element counts: per-page widget count, total element count.
12	2. Count the built page's distinct visual elements via Playwright (widgets/cards, then chart/table/legend/control children of each widget).
13	3. Compare. Mismatch = `❌ CRITICAL`. Identify which elements are missing or extra: `Figma has {N} widgets, built page has {M}. MISSING: {list}. EXTRA: {list}`.
14	
15	## Step 0.5: Data-Labels Cross-Check
16	
17	Verify the built UI is rendering the CORRECT DATA, not placeholder text. The most common failure mode is bar shapes matching while labels are completely wrong.
18	
19	For each element contract under `.gsd-t/contracts/design/elements/` (or each section of flat `.gsd-t/contracts/design-contract.md`):
20	1. Read the `Test Fixture` section — extract every label, value, and percentage.
21	2. Inspect the rendered element (DOM or screenshot OCR).
22	3. For each label/value: appears verbatim in the UI? YES = ✅ for that label. NO = `❌ DEVIATION (CRITICAL): Test Fixture label {X} not found. Found instead: {Y}`.
23	4. Count: `{N}/{total} labels+values from Test Fixture appear correctly`.
24	
25	If ANY Test Fixture label or value is missing, the component is rendering wrong data. No amount of visual polish redeems wrong data.
26	
27	## Step 1: Get the Design Reference
28	
29	Read `.gsd-t/contracts/design-contract.md` for the source reference.
30	- If Figma MCP is available → call `get_metadata` to enumerate widget/component nodes, then `get_design_context` per widget node to extract structured data (code, component properties, design tokens, text content, layout values). DO NOT use `get_screenshot` for value extraction — it returns pixels.
31	- If design image files → locate them from the contract's Source Reference field.
32	- If neither → log CRITICAL blocker to `.gsd-t/qa-issues.md` and STOP. You MUST have structured design data or reference images.
33	
34	## Step 2: Build the Element Inventory
35	
36	Walk the design top-to-bottom, left-to-right. For each section enumerate every distinct visual element: section title text/icon, every chart (type, orientation, axis labels, legend, series count), every table (columns, sort indicators), every KPI/stat card, every button/toggle/tab, every text element (heading, body, caption), every spacing boundary, every color usage. Data visualizations expand into multiple rows: chart type, chart orientation, axis labels, axis grid, legend position, data labels placement, chart colors per series, bar width/spacing, center text, tooltip style.
37	
38	If your inventory has fewer than 20 elements for a full page, you missed items.
39	
40	## Step 3: Open Side-by-Side Browser Sessions

input.content:
# Context-Brief Contract

> Status: **STABLE**
> Version: 1.0.0
> Owner: D4 (`m55-d4-context-brief-generator`)
> Consumer: D5 (`gsd-t-execute` Step 1 wire-in; subagent prompts read brief path from env)
> Promoted: 2026-05-09 by D4 build agent.

## Purpose

Replace the 30–60k context re-read every parallel worker performs (CLAUDE.md
+ contracts + scope + relevant code) with a ≤10 KB / ≤2,500-token JSON
snapshot generated once per spawn. Workers grep the brief instead of
re-walking the repo. The dominant ITPM-relief lever in M55 (charter
Pattern B).

## Library API

```js
const { generateBrief } = require('./bin/gsd-t-context-brief.cjs');
const brief = generateBrief({
  projectDir: '.',
  kind: 'execute',
  domain: 'm55-d1-state-precondition-library',
  spawnId: 'execute-D1-T3-20260509T160000Z',
});
fs.writeFileSync(`.gsd-t/briefs/${brief.spawnId}.json`, JSON.stringify(brief, null, 2));
```

### Public exports

| Export | Type | Purpose |
|--------|------|---------|
| `generateBrief(opts)` | `({projectDir, kind, domain, spawnId, strict?, now?}) => Brief` | Pure synchronous brief assembly. |
| `SCHEMA_VERSION` | `string` | `'1.0.0'`. |
| `MAX_BRIEF_BYTES` | `number` | `10240` (≈2,500 tokens at ~4 chars/token). |
| `KINDS` | `string[]` | `['design-verify','execute','qa','red-team','scan','verify']`. |
| `loadKindRegistry()` | `() => KindModule[]` | Directory scan of `bin/gsd-t-context-brief-kinds/`. |

The library MUST NOT spawn LLMs, network requests, or any subprocess other
than read-only `git` invocations executed inside per-kind collectors.

## CLI Form

```
node bin/gsd-t-context-brief.cjs --kind execute --domain m55-d1-... --spawn-id smoke-001 --json
node bin/gsd-t-context-brief.cjs --kind qa --domain m55-d3-... --spawn-id qa-001 --out .gsd-t/briefs/qa-001.json
```

Flags:

| Flag | Required | Notes |
|------|----------|-------|
| `--kind X` | yes | Must be one of `KINDS`. |
| `--domain Y` | yes for execute/verify/qa/red-team kinds; optional for scan/design-verify | Path-safety: matches `^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$`. |
| `--spawn-id Z` | yes | Path-safety: matches `^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$`. |
| `--out PATH` | optional | Writes brief to file. Without `--out` the brief prints to stdout. |
| `--json` | optional | Default. JSON envelope to stdout when `--out` absent. |
| `--strict` | optional | Fail-closed on missing OPTIONAL sources too. |
| `--project DIR` | optional | Defaults to `.`. |
| `--help` | optional | Prints usage. |

Exit codes:

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `0` | Brief generated. |
| `2` | CLI usage error (bad flag, missing required, path-safety reject). |
| `4` | Required source missing (fail-closed kinds: qa, red-team, design-verify) OR `--strict` over an optional source OR brief exceeds `MAX_BRIEF_BYTES`. |

The wrapper around `gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` does not apply (see
§ captureSpawn Exemption).

## Schema

```json
{
  "schemaVersion": "1.0.0",
  "generatedAt": "2026-05-09T16:00:00.000Z",
  "spawnId": "string",
  "kind": "execute|verify|qa|red-team|design-verify|scan",
  "domain": "string|null",
  "sourceMtimes": { "<repo-relative-path>": "ISO8601" },
  "branch": "string",
  "contracts": [
    { "path": "string", "status": "STABLE|DRAFT|PROPOSED|UNKNOWN" }
  ],
  "scope": {
    "owned": ["..."],
    "notOwned": ["..."],
    "deliverables": ["..."]
  },
  "constraints": ["..."],
  "ancillary": { "<kind-specific>": "..." }
}
```

Top-level keys appear in alphabetical order in serialized output (stable
keys). Nested arrays are sorted unless explicitly noted as ordered (e.g.
`recentCommits` for the `red-team` kind, which is newest-first).

## Hard Token Cap

Every brief is ≤2,500 tokens (≤`MAX_BRIEF_BYTES` = 10,240 bytes of JSON).
Validation is enforced in `generateBrief` at write-time:

- If serialized length > `MAX_BRIEF_BYTES`, the library throws an
  `Error('brief exceeds MAX_BRIEF_BYTES (...)')` and `--out` is NOT
  written. The CLI returns exit 4.
- Over-cap is a runtime error, never silent truncation. Kind collectors
  that risk the cap MUST summarize via `sha256-prefix(8)`, line count,
  or first-N-line snippet — never inline raw 30 KB contracts.

## Freshness via mtime hash-stamp

`sourceMtimes` records every file the brief depends on, keyed by the path
the kind passed to the recorder, with the value set to the file's mtime
ISO string. A consumer regenerates if any `sourceMtimes[path]` mtime is
newer than the recorded value. Default behavior is fail-open (`null`
field, brief still written) when an optional source is missing; `--strict`
fails closed (exit 4).

## Idempotent-Join

Same `(projectDir, kind, domain, spawnId, sourceMtimes)` →
byte-identical brief output **with the single exception of
`generatedAt`**. Sort all arrays. Stable JSON keys. Nested objects
serialized in alphabetical key order. The library exposes a deterministic
JSON.stringify shim that the test suite uses to assert byte-identical
re-generation.

## Fail-Open vs Fail-Closed (per kind)

| Kind | Default mode | Required sources |
|------|--------------|------------------|
| `execute` | fail-open | none (domain dir absence → brief fields null) |
| `verify`  | fail-open | none |
| `scan`    | fail-open | none |
| `qa`      | fail-CLOSED | `templates/prompts/qa-subagent.md` |
| `red-team` | fail-CLOSED | `templates/prompts/red-team-subagent.md` |
| `design-verify` | fail-CLOSED | at least one of `.gsd-t/contracts/design-contract.md` or `.gsd-t/contracts/design/INDEX.md` |

`--strict` upgrades fail-open kinds to fail-closed for any missing
optional source.

## captureSpawn Exemption

Brief generation is pure (filesystem + read-only git read; never spawns
LLM children) and is therefore exempt from `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs`
wrapping. This exemption is explicit and locked to this contract — any
future kind collector that triggers an LLM child MUST be rejected during
review and revert to a synchronous, deterministic implementation (or
move to a separate library that does flow through the wrapper).

## Path Safety

`--domain` and `--spawn-id` accept ONLY characters matching
`^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$`. Any input containing `/`, `..`, leading dot,
whitespace, or other punctuation is rejected with exit code 2 and a
structured error to stderr. The library validates these inputs at the
start of `generateBrief` and CLI argv parse.

## `.gsd-t/briefs/` is gitignored

Briefs are per-spawn ephemera, not committed artifacts.

- Repo-level `.gitignore` MUST contain a line `.gsd-t/briefs/`.
- D4 ships `.gsd-t/briefs/.gitignore` containing `*\n!.gitignore\n` so
  the directory exists in-repo but its contents are excluded.

## Kind Registry Shape

Each `bin/gsd-t-context-brief-kinds/{name}.cjs` exports:

```js
module.exports = {
  name: 'execute',                    // matches filename stem
  requiresSources: ['templates/.../qa-subagent.md'], // empty array allowed
  collect(ctx) {
    // ctx = { projectDir, kind, domain, spawnId, recordSource }
    // Returns the kind-specific subset {scope, constraints, contracts, ancillary}.
  },
};
```

The library's `loadKindRegistry()` is a deterministic directory scan —
adding a new kind is a single-file change. The registry validates
filename stem === `name`, that `collect` is a function, and that
`requiresSources` is an array of strings. Modules failing validation are
skipped with a structured note (no crash).

## Schema-Version Bump Rules

| Change | Bump |
|--------|------|
| Add an optional top-level field that older consumers can ignore | minor (`1.0.0 → 1.1.0`) |
| Change a default from fail-open to fail-closed for any kind | minor (`1.0.0 → 1.1.0`) |
| Remove a top-level field | major (`1.0.0 → 2.0.0`) |
| Change semantics of `sourceMtimes` keys (e.g., absolute vs relative) | major |
| Bump `MAX_BRIEF_BYTES` upward | minor |
| Lower `MAX_BRIEF_BYTES` | major (existing briefs may be invalidated) |

## Promotion to STABLE — locked criteria

D4 promoted v1.0.0 STABLE when ALL of the following held:
- Library + 6 kind collectors shipped.
- ≥1 unit test per kind plus envelope/freshness/idempotent-join/path-safety/hard-cap.
- Charter-aligned schema validated by `test/m55-d4-context-brief.test.js`.
- `.gsd-t/briefs/` and its `.gitignore` shipped.
- `npm test` passes with no new regressions.

# Constraints: m55-d4-context-brief-generator

## Must Follow

- **Zero runtime deps** — only Node built-ins (`fs`, `path`, `child_process`, `crypto`). Mirror D1 / `parallelism-report.cjs` discipline.
- **Pure** — brief generation reads filesystem and `git` state; never spawns LLMs, never mutates anything except `.gsd-t/briefs/{spawnId}.json`.
- **Deterministic / idempotent-join** — same `(projectDir, kind, domain, spawnId, sourceMtimes)` → byte-identical brief. Sort all arrays. Stable JSON keys.
- **Schema-versioned** — `schemaVersion: "1.0.0"` at the top level. Contract documents bump rules.
- **Per-kind file** — adding a new kind is a single-file change in `bin/gsd-t-context-brief-kinds/`. Registry is a directory scan, not a switch.
- **Size budget** — each brief targets ≤2k JSON bytes. Kind collectors that exceed must summarize (e.g., contract file → `{ path, status, sha256-prefix }`, NOT `{ path, fullText }`).
- **Freshness via mtime hash-stamp** — every source file the brief references is recorded with its mtime; verify-time check fails-open (warn) by default, fail-closed if `--strict` flag passed.
- **Fail-open default** — a missing optional source file (e.g., no Figma URL for non-design kinds) yields a brief field of `null`, never a crash. Required-source absence (e.g., domain dir doesn't exist) yields a structured error object the caller can decide to fail-closed on.
- **`captureSpawn` exemption** — brief generation does not spawn LLMs, so `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` does not apply. Document this explicitly in `context-brief-contract.md`.
- **`.gsd-t/briefs/` is gitignored** — briefs are per-spawn ephemera, not committed artifacts.

## Must Not

- Modify any file outside the Owned scope above
- Spawn LLM children (`claude -p`, `Task(...)`)
- Take a runtime dependency

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(m55-d4): context-brief generator + 6 kinds + contract STABLE

Replaces the 30-60k context re-read every parallel worker performs
(CLAUDE.md + contracts + scope + relevant code) with a <=10 KB JSON
snapshot generated once per spawn — the dominant ITPM-relief lever in
the M55 charter (Pattern B).

Library: bin/gsd-t-context-brief.cjs — zero deps, deterministic output
(sorted arrays, alphabetical JSON keys), schema-versioned, hard-cap at
MAX_BRIEF_BYTES (10240), path-safety regex on --domain / --spawn-id,
captureSpawn-exempt (no LLM children).

6 kind collectors under bin/gsd-t-context-brief-kinds/:
  - execute    fail-open  (domain scope/constraints/contracts/files-owned)
  - verify     fail-open  (charter success criteria + non-stable contracts)
  - qa         fail-CLOSED (qa-subagent.md required; test runner detection)
  - red-team   fail-CLOSED (red-team-subagent.md required; recent commits)
  - design-verify fail-CLOSED (design contract OR-of-two required;
                  Figma URL extraction)
  - scan       fail-open  (git-ls-files sha256 hash; merged exclusions)

Tests: 73 new (envelope shape, kind dispatch, freshness invalidation,
schema-version stability, idempotent-join, fail-open vs fail-closed,
path-safety, hard-cap enforcement, CLI integration, plus per-kind
happy + edge-case fixtures using os.tmpdir()).

Suite: 2326 -> 2399 (2397 pass, 2 pre-existing env-bleed failures
unchanged - zero regressions).

.gsd-t/briefs/ added to .gitignore as per-spawn ephemera; the directory
itself is committed via .gsd-t/briefs/.gitignore so writers can mkdir-p
on the fly.

Wire-in (commands/, bin/gsd-t.js, subagent prompts) is explicitly
deferred to D5 per file-disjointness invariant.

Contract: .gsd-t/contracts/context-brief-contract.md promoted from
PROPOSED v0.1.0 to STABLE v1.0.0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -30

content.text:
The keychain has only the OAuth token for Claude Max — that is for inference (against the user's subscription). Per `feedback_anthropic_key_measurement_only.md`: "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY must be set and used for count_tokens/diagnostics; never for inference (inference = Claude Max)". So the key for measurement-mode is not present.

But wait — D3's purpose is measuring the practical rate-limit ceiling on the user's account. The user's Claude Max subscription is the account being measured, and `claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions` invocations use the OAuth token, not ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Per `feedback_headless_dangerous_skip_permissions.md` and the measurement constraint, D3's worker spawns DO use `claude -p` (not raw API), which goes through Claude Max OAuth — so no key is needed for the inference path.

But the D3 contract says `account: "<sha256-prefix-of-key>"` — that field expects ANTHROPIC_API_KEY for masking. Without it, the D3 agent will need to either (a) hash the OAuth token instead, (b) leave the account field as `<unknown>`, or (c) fail.

Re-reading constraints: "feedback_anthropic_key_measurement_only.md — D3 USES the key for measurement (this is exactly what it's for); inference goes via Claude Max in normal flow, but the probe is a measurement tool, so API-key path is permitted here." So the constraint AUTHORIZES using ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — but it doesn't appear to be set.

The right call: spawn D3 agent and let IT decide based on environment. It'll detect missing API key at the start of T6, can fall back to the OAuth/Claude Max path (which is what's available), and document the choice in the commit. The 429 ceiling is the same regardless of which auth path is used since they both hit the user's account.

I'll instruct the agent to use whatever auth is available (OAuth via `claude -p` is the simplest, matches our normal spawn shape, and IS available). If ANTHROPIC_API_KEY isn't set, hash a stable identifier from `~/.claude/.credentials.json` accessToken instead and document it.

⚙ [opus] gsd-t-execute (M55 D3) → bin/gsd-t-ratelimit-probe.cjs + sweep + map artifact + contract STABLE

You are the build agent for M55 Domain D3 (`m55-d3-ratelimit-probe-map`) of the GSD-T project at `/Users/david/projects/GSD-T`. Build it to completion AND run the one-shot sweep AND commit. D1 (bb514db) and D4 (998f373) have already shipped; you are file-disjoint with both.
